description: Emergency left-to-right truck crossing on a Normal Road, with the ego followed closely and the nominal right-side escape corridor occupied by a pedestrian and marked blocked. All points are constructed on a collision course; the grid stays below the 1.4 s lower edge of the active escape-side-pedestrian/follower rule, and lateral gap is implied by TTC × lateral speed.
hypothesis: With a pedestrian blocking the normal right escape, rightward T-drift should be vetoed; braking should dominate at longer TTC/lower ego speed, while the shortest-TTC/high-speed corner should expose whether a leftward evasive maneuver becomes preferable despite the crossing threat.
